notes: This financial statement covers only the ISC-funded programs department of Brokenhead Ojibway Nation, not the full consolidated First Nation. This is the first year the department maintained separate financial records; no comparative figures available. Revenue recognized is $25,315,255 after deferring $18,883,764 to subsequent year and recognizing $8,163,659 deferred from prior year. An operating deficit of $4,343,292 was covered by a transfer from Brokenhead Ojibway Nation. Deferred revenue balance of $18,883,764 primarily relates to capital projects including CFS Capital ($4,563,350), Jordan's Principle - Quonset ($2,515,835), Social Services ($2,245,311), and Community Development Wrap Around ($1,696,522). Auditor firm name not explicitly stated beyond 'Chartered Professional Accountants' with Winnipeg address. Tangible capital assets funded by this department are capitalized in other BON department records, not here.
warnings: High ISC dependency (142.35%) — operations heavily reliant on federal transfers
